Electromechanical System Engineer- CARGOSCAN

Apply Now Save Job

Electromechanical System Engineer- CARGOSCAN

Location Oslo, Oslo County, Norway Date posted June 16, 2026 Job ID 22334
Apply Now Save Job
Our Opening and Your Responsibilities

Mettler-Toledo Cargoscan is a world leader in developing and manufacturing dimensioning equipment and automatic data capture solutions for the Transport and Logistics Industry.

Since 1985 CARGOSCAN has supplied dimensioning and data capture solutions to logistics companies all over the world. We design, develop and manufacture volume measurement instruments that scans three-dimensional items like small parcels on conveyor belt, freight on pallets etc. The instruments are based on visible light laser time-of-flight scanners and represent the "gold standard" in our industry.

CARGOSCAN officially became part of the METTLER TOLEDO group in 2000. We are located in Oslo and serve customers worldwide through Mettler Toledo's global sales and service network. As a strong technological leader, our company offers stability and the potential for exciting career opportunities.

Role Description: 

In this role, you will design and develop cutting-edge solutions for our advanced dimensioning products. You will actively participate in requirements of analysis, craft innovative system architectures, and execute detailed system designs. 

Your work will involve creating and developing integrated electrical and mechanical systems that not only meet but exceed performance, quality, and cost goals. Looking ahead, this role offers the exciting opportunity to grow into a Technical Lead position, where you will be able to lead a small team responsible for delivering innovative system designs. 

Join us to drive next-generation automation and shape intelligent systems that make a real impact! 


Key Responsibilities: 

  • Develop detailed system designs, integrating mechanical and electrical elements. (e.g. dimensioning, scale, barcode scanners, PC, camera, etc.) 
  • Create and review engineering drawings, schematics, and 3D models using mechanical and electrical CAD design software. 
  • Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including mechanical, electrical, and software engineering. 
  • Conduct design analysis, simulations, and validation testing to ensure system functionality and reliability. 
  • Assist in prototyping, production, and product launch activities. 
  • Document design processes, specifications, and test results. 
  • Ensure compliance with applicable industry standards, safety regulations, and quality requirements. 
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives in design methodologies and processes. 
  • Expected Travel: 20 % – 25% 
What You Need to Succeed
Qualifications: 
  • Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering, Electrical engineering, Mechatronics, Systems Engineering, or related field. 
  • Alternatively, Technical Vocational School and extensive hands-on experience in systems design and testing. 
  • Around 5 – 10 years of proven experience in Electrical & Mechanical system design 
  • Proficiency with CAD tools (e.g., Autodesk Inventor, AutoCAD) and electrical design software (e.g., Eplan). 
  • Strong understanding of electrical systems and circuits and mechanical design principles 
  • Experience with sensor integration (LiDAR, 3D cameras or load cells) and physical constraints of high-speed data transmission between them. 
  • Good understanding or experience working in SAP PLM. 
  • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. 
  • Effective communication and teamwork skills. 
  • Knowledge of Declaration of Conformity and approvals such as CE, HAR, UL, NFPA etc. 

Personal skills: 
  • Excellent interpersonal and written/oral skills 
  • Strong organizational skills with a proven ability to effectively manage multiple priorities 
  • Strong analytical abilities (root cause). 
  • Curiosity, creativity and a willingness to go the extra mile. 
  • Ability and willingness to learn new technologies to adapt to industry trends. 
  • Fluent English communication skills. Norwegian language is a plus. 
Our Offer to You
  • A position in a highly skilled, multicultural team.  
  • A brand recognized worldwide for precision, quality, and innovation.   
  • A role in a growing market where strong initiatives really make a difference.   
  • Group life insurance ☂️ 
  • Pension: 7%  
  • Health insurance 
  • Business travel insurance ✈️   
  • Cantina: salad bar and warm meals 2x per week 
  • Free parking
  • Free charging for electric cars ⚡ 
  • Network building at a global company: a global network of resources and expertise, with opportunities to travel  

    • For more information about Transport & Logistics in METTLER TOLEDO visit www.mt.com/logistics-competency

About Mettler Toledo
METTLER TOLEDO is a global leader in precision instruments and services. We are renowned for innovation and quality across laboratory, process analytics, industrial, product inspection, and retailing applications. Our sales and service network is one of the most extensive in the industry. Our products are sold in more than 140 countries, and we have a direct presence in approximately 40 countries. For more information, please visit www.mt.com.
Equal Opportunity Employment
We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We give consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, or any other protected classification. You can find more details in our Equal Employment Opportunity Policy.
